evals.prompt: We currently use Fastlane supply for our store listings and the metadata is at fastlane/metadata/android/ with en-US, ja-JP, and fr-FR translations. We want to switch to gpc for everything. Can we keep using the same directory structure or do we need to convert something?
evals.expected_output: Confirms Fastlane metadata format is directly compatible, shows how to push existing metadata with GPC
expectations: Confirms GPC reads Fastlane metadata directory format natively
Mentions gpc listings pull for downloading current listings
Notes that the same directory structure works for both tools

evals.prompt: I need to update our app screenshots for the phone category in en-US. I have 5 new PNGs in ~/screenshots/ and I want to replace all the existing screenshots. What's the process?
evals.expected_output: Shows how to list existing screenshots, delete old ones, and upload new ones
expectations: Shows gpc listings images list to see existing images
Shows gpc listings images delete to remove old screenshots
Shows gpc listings images upload with the correct --type phoneScreenshots
Notes image format requirements (PNG/JPEG, size limits)

evals.prompt: Our app supports 12 languages and the product team wants to pull all current listings into git so translators can work on them in PRs. Then we need a way to push changes back after review. How should we set this up?
evals.expected_output: Describes a pull → edit → PR → push workflow using GPC with version-controlled metadata
expectations: Shows gpc listings pull --dir metadata/ to download all languages
Explains the directory structure that gets created (lang/title.txt, etc.)
Suggests committing the pulled metadata to git
Shows gpc listings push --dir metadata/ --dry-run for previewing
Recommends using --dry-run in CI to validate before pushing
